skill.name: page-builder
skill.description: Guides building landing pages and marketing sites with Tailark components, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI patterns.
skill.summary: Build marketing pages by selecting and composing Tailark UI sections.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown inline code and component reference text only. No executable commands, cryptographic operations, system probing, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown References
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptographic algorithm, and system reconnaissance findings are Markdown inline code or ordinary component reference text. SKILL.md describes workflow steps and reference.md lists Tailark component names, file paths, layouts, and recommendations. No executable code path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown documentation, and the flagged lines are inline code spans, filenames, headings, or descriptive words. There is no shell execution syntax outside documentation text and no code that can run these strings.

content.value_statement: Creating polished marketing pages can be slow when teams must choose sections from many UI components. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to select and compose Tailark sections for common page types.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Tailark components for landing, pricing, about, and contact pages.
Maps page sections to documented component files such as hero, features, testimonials, pricing, FAQ, and footer sections.
Guides composition of selected components inside a semantic page structure.
Suggests when to copy and customize component code for text, images, or layout changes.
Calls out component complexity, dependencies, and best-fit usage based on the included reference.
limitations: Does not include the Tailark component source files themselves.
Assumes a project already supports Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI, and related dependencies.
Does not implement authentication flows and recommends a separate auth handler for those pages.
Does not verify visual output or run application tests by itself.

use_cases.title: Launch a SaaS Landing Page
use_cases.description: Select a hero, logo cloud, feature section, testimonials, call to action, and footer for a conversion-focused homepage.

use_cases.title: Create a Pricing Experience
use_cases.description: Choose between simple pricing and comparison-table layouts, then pair the result with FAQ content.

use_cases.title: Assemble Company Pages
use_cases.description: Combine content, statistics, team, and contact sections for about and support pages.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Page Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the page-builder skill to recommend Tailark sections for a landing page about [product]. Include the component names and page order.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Page Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a [landing/pricing/about/contact] page using Tailark components. Explain why each section fits the audience and conversion goal.

prompt_templates.title: Customize Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the page-builder skill, show how to compose the selected Tailark sections in page.tsx and identify which components need copied customization.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Marketing Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing marketing page structure: [sections]. Use the Tailark reference to recommend replacements, missing sections, and simplifications.

output_examples.input: Recommend sections for a new analytics SaaS homepage.
output: Hero section for the main value proposition.
Dashboard feature section for product depth.
Call to action and footer for conversion.

output_examples.input: I need a pricing page with several plan differences.
output: Use the pricing comparator for plan comparison.
Add FAQ content below the table.
Use a footer for secondary navigation and legal links.

output_examples.input: Create an about page for a small team.
output: Use an editorial content section for the story.
Show the team section for trust.
End with contact information or a call to action.

best_practices: Start with the page goal, audience, and required sections before choosing components.
Replace placeholder images, copy, and icons with real product assets.
Prefer simpler component variants unless the page needs advanced visuals or interactions.
anti_patterns: Do not import every Tailark section when a focused page needs only a few sections.
Do not use authentication components from this skill; use a dedicated auth handler instead.
Do not keep placeholder copy, placeholder images, or generic testimonials in production pages.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build landing pages, pricing pages, about pages, contact pages, and related marketing sections.

faq.question: Does it generate Tailark component source code?
faq.answer: It provides selection and composition guidance. The actual component source must exist in the target project.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it assume?
faq.answer: It assumes a React or Next.js project using Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI, lucide-react, and Next image patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with pricing pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends simple pricing layouts or comparison tables and pairs them with FAQ sections.

faq.question: Can it build authentication pages?
faq.answer: No. The reference marks login, sign-up, and password reset pages as better handled by an auth-specific skill.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files are Markdown guidance and component references with no executable behavior.
